attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Luminous Blade
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Volcanic Heat
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Remove all effects of attacks on each player and his or her Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Despair Ray
attacks.effect: Discard as many of your Benched Pokémon as you like. This attack does 10 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Luminous Blade
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Soul Blaster
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Soul Blaster attack's base damage is 60.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Canyon Axe
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Edge
attacks.effect: You may do 50 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage times the number of heads.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Volcanic Heat
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Steelix-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Shuffle 2 Special Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Remove all effects of attacks on each player and his or her Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and switch it with that Pokémon. (Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle the first Pokémon into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only if you took it as a face-down Prize card, before you put it into your hand.

Flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gardevoir-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Anorith you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent's Bench.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Shieldon you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

attacks.name: Aero Blitz
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: When attacking prey, it can reach speeds of up to 310 mph. It finishes its prey off with a colossal kick.
The hotter the flame sac on its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going.
Despite the beauty of its lilting voice, it's merciless to intruders that enter its territory.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Sky Drop
attacks.effect: This attack does 120 damage minus 20 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger.

attacks.name: Furry Chance
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

attacks.name: Fiddle Around
attacks.effect: Look at the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved from Meowth during this turn, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

attacks.name: Stall
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

attacks.name: Calamity Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Hydreigon BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Cruel Fang
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Dark Burn
attacks.effect: Discard as many Darkness Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Darkness Energy you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It responds to movement by attacking. This scary, three-headed Pokémon devours everything in its path!

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Power Breath
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Lacking sight, it's unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

attacks.name: Proud Fang
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Pokémon BREAK in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Claw
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

attacks.name: Life Stream
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Xerneas BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Geomancy
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fairy Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Rainbow Spear
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.
It never lets go of a key that it likes, so people give it the keys to vaults and safes as a way to prevent crime.

attacks.name: Despair Ray
attacks.effect: Discard as many of your Benched Pokémon as you like. This attack does 10 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Luminous Blade
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it's in the water.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.

attacks.name: Soul Blaster
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Soul Blaster attack's base damage is 60.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Quick Guard
attacks.effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. This Pokémon can't use Quick Guard during your next turn.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

flavorText: It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gear Spinner
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Gear Spinner attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

attacks.name: Disorderly Flip
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A minigear and big gear comprise its body. If the minigear it launches at a foe doesn't return, it will die.

attacks.name: Disorderly Flip
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

attacks.name: Counter Head
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fortress of Rage
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

flavorText: Any frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.

attacks.name: Rock Head
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Armor Fossil Shieldon
flavorText: It was generated from a fossil dug out of a layer of clay that was older than anyone knows. It has a sturdy face.

attacks.name: Canyon Axe
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Edge
attacks.effect: You may do 50 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage times the number of heads.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Baleful Night
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Yveltal BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Oblivion Wing
attacks.effect: Attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Darkness Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

attacks.name: Charge Order
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pawniard.

flavorText: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Damage Play
attacks.effect: Move as many damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent's other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.
They live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.

attacks.name: Nyan Roll
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

attacks.name: Poison Up
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, put 3 more damage counters on that Pokémon between turns. This effect can be applied more than once.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Rushing Water
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, its body is very sturdy. It skewers prey with its claws to feed.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Claw Fossil Anorith
flavorText: A Pokémon ancestor that was reanimated from a fossil. It lived in the sea and hunted with claws.

attacks.name: Bounce Back
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rock Tomb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its magnetic nose consistently faces north. Travelers check Nosepass to get their bearings.

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spirited Throw
attacks.effect: If, before doing damage, your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It is always outrageously furious. If it gives chase, it will tenaciously track the target no matter how far.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Flop attack's base damage is 50.

flavorText: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

attacks.name: Hyperspace Punch
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Portal Strike
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Portal Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.

attacks.name: Past Friends
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile.

flavorText: The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever.

attacks.name: Mirror Mirror
attacks.effect: Each player either draws or discards cards until that player has 4 cards in his or her hand. (Your opponent does this first.)

attacks.name: Flickering Flames
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It arrives near the moment of death and steals spirit from the body.

attacks.name: Slightly Simmer
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and discard them. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

attacks.name: Eerie Wave
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Burst Curse
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. Put 8 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It's drowsy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

attacks.name: Transfer Pain
attacks.effect: Move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon are called the "Signpost for Wandering Spirits." Children holding them sometimes vanish.

attacks.name: Power Lariat
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Evolution Pokémon on your Bench.

flavorText: One swing of its mighty tail can snap a telephone pole as if it were a matchstick.

attacks.name: Nido Press
attacks.effect: If Nidorina is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

attacks.name: Come Along
attacks.effect: Search your deck for Nidoran ♀ and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its large ears are flapped like wings when it is listening to distant sounds. It extends toxic barbs when angered.

attacks.name: Double Thread
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. Also apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electroweb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.
They attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in an electric pouch.

attacks.name: Gigavolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Melting Floe
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. For each Water Energy card you discarded in this way, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: The way several Bergmite huddle on its back make it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice.

attacks.name: Ice Block
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Using air of -150 degrees Fahrenheit, they freeze opponents solid. They live in herds above the snow line on mountains.

attacks.name: Lock-On
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn. During your next turn, any damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is increased by 120 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Clawitzer BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

flavorText: By expelling water from the nozzle in the back of its claw, it can move at a speed of 60 knots.

attacks.name: Double Pincers
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Through controlled expulsions of internal gas, it can expel water like a pistol shot. At close distances, it can shatter rock.

attacks.name: Ultimate Blade
attacks.effect: If the damage from this attack reduces your opponent's Active Pokémon's HP to 60 or less, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Pike
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

attacks.name: Razor Shell
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: As a result of strict training, each Dewott learns different forms for using the scalchops.
It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

attacks.name: Sticky Shot
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It apparently had a huge shell for protection in ancient times. It lives in shallow tidal pools.
Its shape and coloration vary, depending on its habitat.

attacks.name: Healing Wave
attacks.effect: Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Dwindling Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 90 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: While elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores Remoraid that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Heater
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to each of those Pokémon.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

attacks.name: Kaiser Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Pyroar BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: With fiery breath of more than 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, they viciously threaten any challenger. The females protect the pride's cubs.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.effect: Discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Talonflame BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.effect: Discard all Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Up
attacks.effect: If you have fewer than 10 Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does nothing. Shuffle 10 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

flavorText: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.
It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.

attacks.name: Returning Flames
attacks.effect: Put 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Its legs grow strong while it chases after its parent. It runs in fields and mountains all day.

attacks.name: Shimmering Scales
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Power Hurricane
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona's fire provided a replacement for the sun.
The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

attacks.name: Crazy Spore
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Strange Reaction
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, it is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.
It lures Pokémon with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball then releases poison spores.

attacks.name: Wicked Wind
attacks.effect: Until the end of your opponent's next turn, each Stadium or Pokémon Tool card in play has no effect. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 ft/second that can level houses.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

attacks.name: Bide
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, if this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's next turn, it is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

flavorText: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

attacks.name: Barrier Break
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Yanmega BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Assault Boom
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

attacks.name: Fluffy Transport
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Solar Step
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the number of your remaining Prize cards.

flavorText: Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.
It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.
It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

attacks.name: Leaf Storm
attacks.effect: Heal 40 damage from each of your Grass Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flog
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can't even see its eyes.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.
